headline: Clean-room, code-only, blind: undirected NAIVE MISSED the decode bug (diagnosed a plausible-but-wrong alternative); decode-completeness METHOD CAUGHT it. method recall 1/1, naive recall 0/1 on this single finding. n=1 -> suggestive, NOT a calibrated rate.

protocol.type: blind, code-only, no-advisory-lookup clean-room (matches the prior SOTA-validation protocol)
protocol.naive_definition: undirected single competent audit pass over the core source; find the single most severe vulnerability; NO hint about decoding/the answer area; code-only, no web
protocol.method_definition: same source, plus the decode-completeness FORCING FUNCTION: for every external/precompile read, recover the full wire schema and check whether any VALUE-REDUCING field is dropped/mis-indexed; trace downstream. (The forcing function is the method, not the answer.)
protocol.scorer: non-LLM: predicted_label vs ground_truth (the sota_bench.scorer discipline)

naive_undirected.what_it_said: Rank1 = settle/distributeYield against MTM/illiquid backing -> peg break (Medium); Rank2 = a spot-vs-supplied DOUBLE-COUNT in backing (reached the 0x811 read but framed it as double-count, NOT the dropped-debt-leg decode); Rank3 = operator-controlled hedge prices. Did NOT identify the dropped borrow leg.
naive_undirected.note: Reached the 0x811 site and ruled out a decode bug, converging on the same plausible-but-wrong alternative class as the SOTA naive finders. The audit was THOROUGH (37 tool calls, multi-file trace) and still missed it.

method.what_it_said: Per-read schema enumeration across all precompile reads identified 0x811 suppliedBalance as the ONLY value-reducing-field-dropped case: takes word[3]=supplied, drops the leading borrow/debt field; traced to totalBackingSigned() over-count -> distributeYield phantom yield. Located PrecompileReader.sol:90.

score_n1.interpretation: On THIS one decode finding the method beats naive on detection. n=1 => suggestive, NOT a rate. To become a calibrated rate this needs >= MIN_SLICE_N (=10) discordant decode findings; the admission floor mechanically blocks promotion until then.

honesty_corrections: DISCARDED a FIRST naive run as CONTAMINATED: its prompt explicitly asked 'is there a bug in how a precompile read is DECODED (a dropped/mis-indexed field)?', leaking the answer direction. Under that leak BOTH naive and method flagged M-01 (naive 0.62), which would have falsely shown no method edge OR a contaminated naive hit. The undirected re-run above is the valid measurement.

The naive baseline here is a THOROUGH multi-file audit, which is a HARDER bar than a strict single-pass 'naive single call'; the method still won, so the edge is the systematic field-enumeration discipline, not naive under-resourcing. If anything, a stricter single-pass naive would be even more likely to miss.

This is the ONE in-hand class where the method wins; the authz_v1 pinned baseline is the inverse anchor where the method LOST (naive 0.833 > method 0.667). Lead with the loss; this decode win is a single UNDERPOWERED datapoint.
admission: NOT registered in SLICES.md (stays Planned/seed). datasets/ does not contain decode_v1.jsonl; this seed lives under seeds/ so CI does not treat it as a shipped slice. admission.py MIN_SLICE_N=10 floor rejects any 1-row slice (disqualifier #6).
